4. Employee Benefits
The Employee shall be entitled to participate in the employee benefit plans maintained by the
Company, subject to the terms and conditions of such plans. These benefits currently include:
Health & Wellness: Comprehensive Medical, Dental, and Vision insurance plans covering
the Employee and their eligible dependents.
Paid Time Off (PTO): Twenty (20) days of accrued Paid Time Off per calendar year, in
addition to recognized company holidays.
Retirement: A 401(k) retirement savings plan with a Company match of up to 4% of the

be payable in equal semi-monthly installments in accordance with the Company’s standard

2.2  Performance  Bonus: The  Employee  shall  be  eligible  for  an  annual  discretionary

Board of Directors.
2.3 Equity/Stock Options: Subject to the approval of the Board of Directors, the Employee will
be granted 5,000 Restricted Stock Units (RSUs), which will vest over a four-year period, with a
one-year cliff of 25%, and monthly vesting thereafter, subject to the terms of the Company's
Equity Incentive Plan.
3. Working Hours and Location
3.1 Working Hours: The Employee is expected to work standard business hours, typically
Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM. However, as an exempt employee, the Employee
may be required to work additional hours as necessary to fulfill their duties.

testCases.name: test_case_7
testCases.input: what is the total potential annual compensation including base salary and maximum bonus ?
testCases.actualOutput:
The base salary is $135,000.00 (Page 1) and the maximum bonus is 15% of the base salary (Page 2). 
The maximum bonus is $135,000 * 0.15 = $20,250. 
The total potential annual compensation is $135,000 + $20,250 = $155,250.
testCases.expectedOutput: it's the sum of the base salary and the maximum bonus which is $135,000.00 + (15% of $135,000.00) = $155,250.00

NexTech Analytics Corp.
EMPLOYEE:

EMPLOYMENT AGREEMENT
NexTech Analytics Corp. | 100 Innovation Drive, Tech City, TX 75001
This Employment Agreement (the "Agreement") is made and entered into as of July 1, 2026 (the
"Effective  Date"),  by  and  between  NexTech  Analytics  Corp.,  a  corporation  organized  and
existing under the laws of the State of Texas (the "Company"), and [Employee Name], residing
at [Employee Address] (the "Employee").
WHEREAS, the Company desires to employ the Employee, and the Employee desires to be
employed by the Company in the capacity of Data Engineer, subject to the terms and conditions
set forth herein.
1. Position and Duties
1.1 Position: The Company agrees to employ the Employee in the position of Data Engineer.
The Employee will report directly to the Chief Technology Officer (CTO) or to another executive
as designated by the Company.
1.2 Responsibilities: The Employee’s primary responsibilities shall include, but are not limited
to:

may be required to work additional hours as necessary to fulfill their duties.
3.2 Location: The Employee will primarily perform their duties remotely (Work-From-Home),
with the requirement to visit the corporate office in Tech City, TX, up to three (3) days per month
for strategic planning and team-building sessions. Travel expenses for required office visits will
be covered by the Company.
4. Employee Benefits
The Employee shall be entitled to participate in the employee benefit plans maintained by the
Company, subject to the terms and conditions of such plans. These benefits currently include:
Health & Wellness: Comprehensive Medical, Dental, and Vision insurance plans covering
the Employee and their eligible dependents.
Paid Time Off (PTO): Twenty (20) days of accrued Paid Time Off per calendar year, in
addition to recognized company holidays.
Retirement: A 401(k) retirement savings plan with a Company match of up to 4% of the
Employee’s base salary.

5.2 Intellectual Property: Any software, algorithms, code, data pipelines, processes, or other
intellectual property developed, created, or improved by the Employee during the term of
employment,  whether  during  or  outside  working  hours,  which  relates  to  the  Company's
business, shall be the sole and exclusive property of the Company.
6. Term and Termination
6.1  At-Will  Employment: The  employment  relationship  between  the  Company  and  the
Employee is "at-will." This means that either the Company or the Employee may terminate the
employment relationship at any time, with or without cause, and with or without advance
notice.
6.2 Severance: If the Company terminates the Employee’s employment without "Cause" (as

notice.
6.2 Severance: If the Company terminates the Employee’s employment without "Cause" (as
defined in the Company's standard policies), the Employee shall be entitled to severance pay
equal to one (1) month of base salary for each completed year of service, up to a maximum of
six (6) months, contingent upon the execution of a standard release of claims.
7. General Provisions
7.1 Governing Law: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the
laws of the State of Texas, without regard to its conflicts of law principles.
7.2 Entire Agreement: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties
regarding the terms and conditions of the Employee’s employment and supersedes all prior
agreements, understandings, and representations, whether written or oral.
IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Employment Agreement as of the
Effective Date written above.
